title: aciphylla montana var. montana
intro: Aciphylla montana var. montana, also known as the mountain parsnip, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Aciphylla montana var. montana include its ability to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, salves, or poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
